sleep.name: Sleep Architecture
sleep.desc: The compounds that protect the master variable. Circadian support, GABA modulation, and core temperature regulation.

items.name: Magnesium L-Threonate
items.why: Sleep stack anchor. Combined with Apigenin and Glycine, this is the recommended pre-sleep protocol. Under the training volume ahead, magnesium loss through sweat is a real risk.
science: Only magnesium form shown to cross the blood-brain barrier effectively
Supports GABA signaling, reduces sleep onset latency
80% of Americans are magnesium deficient

sources.title: Magnesium L-threonate enhances learning and memory (original study)
Magnesium and sleep quality (systematic review)
L-threonate form premium pricing vs standard magnesium forms (cost-effectiveness analysis)
Examine.com analysis: most magnesium sleep benefits apply to deficient populations

items.rationale: Heavy training + sweat + deficit increases depletion risk. L-threonate specifically targets the CNS.
items.impact: Sleep onset latency, deep sleep %, cognitive clarity
items.watching: Eight Sleep score on adherent vs non-adherent nights
items.hoped_outcome: Shorter sleep-onset latency and a higher deep-sleep share on adherent nights — the sleep stack's anchor claim.
items.measured_by: Eight Sleep onset latency + deep-sleep % on adherent vs non-adherent nights; Whoop sleep performance as the cross-check.

items.name: Glycine
items.why: Beautiful synergy with Eight Sleep — both work by lowering core temperature. Collagen synthesis bonus for joint health during heavy training.
science: 2-3g before bed lowers core body temperature
Primary physiological trigger for sleep onset
Synergistic with Eight Sleep cooling

sources.title: Glycine ingestion improves subjective sleep quality (original RCT)
Glycine-induced hypothermia and sleep regulation
Effect size on sleep is modest in non-deficient populations

items.rationale: Synergistic with Eight Sleep cooling — both lower core temp via different mechanisms.
items.impact: Core temp drop, sleep onset latency, deep sleep %
items.hoped_outcome: A faster core-temperature drop at bedtime, showing up as shorter sleep-onset latency.
items.measured_by: Eight Sleep bed-temperature and sleep-onset data — the same instrument the mechanism acts on.

items.name: Apigenin
items.why: Gentlest member of the sleep stack — best risk/reward ratio. If you take one sleep supplement, this is it.
science: Flavonoid that modulates GABA-A receptors
Reduces sleep onset latency and anxiety without grogginess
No tolerance, no dependence

sources.title: Apigenin as a GABA-A receptor modulator (preclinical)
Chamomile extract (apigenin) for generalized anxiety disorder
Limited human clinical trials — most evidence is preclinical

items.rationale: Best risk/reward in the sleep stack — extremely safe with plausible mechanism.
items.impact: Sleep onset latency, subjective sleep quality
items.hoped_outcome: Calmer sleep onset as part of the pre-bed stack — tested as a unit with magnesium and glycine, not expected to be separable on its own.
items.measured_by: Eight Sleep sleep-score trend on stack-adherent vs non-adherent nights.

items.name: L-Theanine
items.why: Monitor your response — if Eight Sleep shows no difference or worse sleep on Theanine nights, candidate for removal.
science: Promotes alpha brain waves, reduces anxiety without sedation
Synergistic with caffeine during the day
Some people get disruptive vivid dreams — individual response varies

sources.title: L-theanine promotes relaxation without drowsiness
Effects of L-theanine on stress and sleep quality
Vivid dreams may disrupt sleep architecture

items.rationale: Monitoring via Eight Sleep — data will determine whether this stays.
items.questionReason: Individual response varies — vivid dreams may disrupt sleep for some
items.hoped_outcome: A calmer wind-down and easier sleep onset without sedation.
items.measured_by: Sleep-onset latency (Eight Sleep) + wind-down and vivid-dream notes in the journal.

metabolic.name: Metabolic & Body Comp
metabolic.desc: Supporting the deficit engine. Glucose management, fat oxidation, and gut health.

items.name: Myo-Inositol
items.why: During metabolic transformation, insulin sensitivity is a key lever. CGM data should validate.
science: Supports insulin signaling and serotonin receptor sensitivity
Anxiolytic properties
Evidence for metabolic benefit in insulin-resistant populations

sources.title: Myo-inositol improves insulin sensitivity (meta-analysis)
Inositol and glucose metabolism in metabolic syndrome
Most positive evidence is in PCOS populations — limited data in metabolically healthy males

items.rationale: CGM data will validate whether glucose disposal improves — clear measurable outcome.
items.impact: Glucose variability, insulin sensitivity via CGM
items.hoped_outcome: Flatter post-meal glucose and lower overall glucose variability.
items.measured_by: CGM post-meal excursions and variability on adherent vs non-adherent stretches.

items.name: Green Tea Phytosome (EGCG)
items.why: Marginal fat oxidation support. Insurance, not primary lever. Weight trend won't show it.
science: EGCG supports fat oxidation and thermogenesis
Antioxidant properties
Modest effect on fat loss (1-2 lbs over 12 weeks)

sources.title: Green tea catechins and fat oxidation (meta-analysis)
EGCG and thermogenesis in humans
Effect size on fat loss is 1-2 lbs over 12 weeks — clinically meaningless

items.rationale: Insurance play only — wouldn't supplement for fat loss alone.
items.questionReason: Effect size too small to measure — pure insurance play
items.hoped_outcome: A marginal fat-oxidation assist — honestly small; no visible signal is the likely outcome.
items.measured_by: The weight-trend rate read against the deficit math; a clear null here retires it.

items.name: Probiotics
items.why: During high-protein deficit, GI comfort matters for adherence. Consider fermented foods instead.
science: Gut microbiome correlates with metabolic health and mood
Evidence is strain-specific and mixed
Fermented foods may be superior for diversity

sources.title: Probiotics and gut microbiome modulation (systematic review)
Probiotics for GI health during dietary restriction
Probiotic supplementation may reduce gut microbiome diversity vs fermented foods

items.rationale: Candidate for replacement with fermented food strategy.
items.watching: GI comfort changes on vs off
items.questionReason: Candidate for reassessment — fermented foods may be food-first approach
items.hoped_outcome: Steadier GI comfort and digestion quality.
items.measured_by: GI-comfort notes in the journal on vs off; no lab marker is expected to move.

cognitive.name: Cognitive Support
cognitive.desc: Neuroplasticity, focus, and the cognitive infrastructure.

items.name: Lion's Mane
items.pausedReason: Not in the starting stack — emerging evidence only, held until adherence can be tracked
items.why: Cognitive performance matters. Emerging evidence — don't stress if missed.
science: Stimulates nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis
Supports neuroplasticity and cognitive function
Not yet confirmed in large RCTs

sources.title: Lion's mane stimulates nerve growth factor synthesis (preclinical)
Hericium erinaceus improves mild cognitive impairment (small RCT)
No large-scale human RCTs — most evidence is preclinical or small samples

items.rationale: Held out of the starting stack. A good N=1 candidate if picked up with real adherence tracking.
items.impact: Subjective cognitive clarity, focus during deep work
items.questionReason: Emerging evidence only — good N=1 candidate
